action.reasoning: Observation: The baseline CLIP ViT-B/16 model achieved 0.3363 worst-region accuracy (Africa) after 3 epochs. The iteration took ~7.5 minutes, which is very fast. Analysis: We have plenty of time budget (9.8 hours) and compute (4x 48GB GPUs). Upgrading to a larger model (CLIP ViT-L/14) and training for more epochs (10) with a proper learning rate schedule (cosine with warmup) and weight decay should significantly improve performance. Decision: Update model.py, train.py, and predict.py to use ViT-L/14. Implement cosine LR scheduler and weight decay in train.py. Launch training with `accelerate launch --num_processes 4` to ensure all 4 GPUs are utilized, then submit for evaluation.
